title: Drag-and-Drop Dashboard Frontend System Design
description:
Design a customizable frontend dashboard where widgets can be dragged, snapped to a grid, resized within constraints, and restored from a saved layout. Focus on the client-side layout model, pointer interaction ownership, collision resolution, rendering performance, persistence, accessibility, and responsive behavior.

Key considerations:
- Grid-based layout data model with logical coordinates instead of raw pixels.
- Drag and resize controllers that use pointer events, pointer capture, snapping, and min-size constraints.
- Collision strategies for rejecting, pushing, swapping, compacting, or allowing overlap.
- requestAnimationFrame-driven visual updates that avoid framework rerenders on every pointermove.
- Optimistic local layout while persisting versioned snapshots to localStorage or a server.
- Keyboard drag/resize affordances and multi-device responsive layout behavior.

You should be prepared to explain the frontend ownership boundary: the layout engine owns grid math and collisions, the interaction layer translates pointer or keyboard input into grid deltas, widget content stays mostly isolated, and persistence commits a versioned layout after interaction end.

seo.title: Drag-and-Drop Dashboard Frontend System Design: Grid Layout, Resize, Collision and Persistence
seo.description: Practice draggable dashboard frontend system design with a grid data model, pointer interactions, collision snapping, rAF rendering, persistence migrations, responsive behavior, and accessibility.
